title: La Fuite De La Lune
content:
To outer senses there is peace,
    A dreamy peace on either hand
    Deep silence in the shadowy land,
    Deep silence where the shadows cease.

    Save for a cry that echoes shrill
    From some lone bird disconsolate;
    A corncrake calling to its mate;
    The answer from the misty hill.

    And suddenly the moon withdraws
    Her sickle from the lightening skies,
    And to her sombre cavern flies,
    Wrapped in a veil of yellow gauze.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Oscar Fingal O'Flahertie Wills Wilde, titled "La Fuite De La Lune", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
